description: Quality rubric for OpenAPI specs. 12 rules across 4 categories. Each rule has a severity, and each severity carries a weight used to compute a 0-100 quality score.

rules.title: Operations are documented
rules.description: Every operation defines both a non-empty summary and a description.
rules.example: Failing: a GET /orders/{id} with no summary or description.

rules.title: Parameters and schema properties are described
rules.description: Every parameter and every schema property has a description.
rules.example: Failing: a `status` query param with no description.

rules.title: Request and response bodies provide examples
rules.description: Request bodies and 2xx responses include at least one example or example value.
rules.example: Failing: a POST /payments with a schema but no sample payload.

rules.title: Security schemes are defined
rules.description: The spec declares at least one security scheme under components.securitySchemes.
rules.example: Failing: a spec with no securitySchemes at all.

rules.title: Operations require authentication
rules.description: Every operation is covered by a security requirement, either globally (root `security`) or per-operation, unless explicitly marked public.
rules.example: Failing: a DELETE /users/{id} with no security applied and no global security.

rules.title: Transport and examples are safe
rules.description: All server URLs use HTTPS, and examples contain no real-looking secrets, tokens, or PII.
rules.example: Failing: `servers: [{ url: 'http://api.example.com' }]` or an example Authorization header containing a live-looking key.

rules.title: Consistent path naming
rules.description: Paths use lowercase, hyphenated, plural-noun resource segments and avoid trailing slashes or verbs in paths.
rules.example: Failing: mixing /orders and /order_items and /getInventory in one spec.

rules.title: Consistent property casing
rules.description: Schema property names use a single consistent casing convention (default: camelCase).
rules.example: Failing: a schema with both `firstName` and `last_name`.

rules.title: Operations have unique operationIds
rules.description: Every operation defines an operationId, and all operationIds are unique within the spec.
rules.example: Failing: two operations both missing operationId, or two sharing 'getItem'.

rules.title: Error responses are declared
rules.description: Each operation declares at least one 4xx response, and mutating operations (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) declare relevant client and server error responses.
rules.example: Failing: a POST /orders that only documents 200.

rules.title: Responses reference a schema
rules.description: Every 2xx response with a body references a schema (inline or via $ref); no empty content or untyped 'object' placeholders.
rules.example: Failing: `200: { description: 'OK' }` with no content, where a body is clearly returned.

rules.title: Spec metadata is complete
rules.description: info.version, info.title, info.contact, and at least one servers[] entry are present.
rules.example: Failing: a spec with no contact and no servers block.
